name: Luciano Manhães de Andrade Filho
title: Prof. Dr.
bio: Associate Professor IV at UFJF and Team Leader of the UFJF group at CERN/ATLAS. B.Sc. in Electrical Engineering (UERJ, 2001), M.Sc. in Scientific Instrumentation (CBPF, 2004, sandwich at CERN), Ph.D. in Electrical Engineering (UFRJ/COPPE, 2009, sandwich at CERN), and two post-doctorates — PLC communications at UFJF (2009) and Nuclear Instrumentation at CERN (2021). Coordinator of the NIPSCERN Lab and member of the INCT CERN-Brasil (Instituto Nacional de Ciência e Tecnologia para Física de Altas Energias com o CERN). Expert in signal processing for calorimetry, FPGA-based systems, and machine learning applied to particle detectors. Web of Science: 1,256 works · 67,244 citations · h-index 118. Scopus: 1,207 works · 78,835 citations · h-index 124. He ranks among the top 0.1% most cited scientists in the world.

awards.title: Most-Liked Video — SEMIC 2025 ("Macro e Microcosmos: Fractais e a Física de Partículas")

name: Chrysthofer Arthur Amaro Afonso
title: B.Sc. Student
bio: Undergraduate researcher at NIPSCERN Lab (UFJF) and CERN collaborator since 2023. FAPEMIG scholar developing extensions to the Lorenzetti framework for 3D calorimeter event visualization and free-running trigger studies (CGV-WEB). Contributes to the SAPHO processor, YANC compilers, POLARIS IDE, and AURORA IDE. Acts as technical co-supervisor (co-orientador) of undergraduate IC students. Member of the INCT CERN-Brasil (Instituto Nacional de Ciência e Tecnologia para Física de Altas Energias com o CERN). Participates in the Nordic Online Logic Seminar (NOLS) and the Young Network for Wittgensteinian Philosophy (YNWP).
awards: Gold Medal — International Physics and Culture Olympiad (IPhCO) (2019, 2021)
Silver Medal — International Physics and Culture Olympiad (IPhCO) (2019, 2021)
Bronze Medal — International Physics and Culture Olympiad (IPhCO) (2019, 2020, 2021)
Gold Medal — Brazilian Astronomy and Astronautics Olympiad (OBA) (2020, 2021)
Gold Medal — Brazilian National Science Olympiad (2021)
Honorable Mention — Brazilian Mathematics Olympiad OBMEP (2018, 2019, 2021)
Most-Liked Video — SEMIC 2025 ("Macro e Microcosmos: Fractais e a Física de Partículas")

name: Gabriel de Aquino Torres
bio: Undergraduate IC scholar (Bolsista CNPq) at NIPSCERN Lab (UFJF), B.Sc. student in Electrical Engineering – Electronic Systems. Contributing to the graphical interface of POLARIS IDE and the YAWT Wave Tracer — SAPHO's signal visualisation tool. Also working on particle detection in calorimetry using deep learning techniques, under supervision of Prof. Dr. Luciano Manhães de Andrade Filho.

name: Ualison Rodrigo Ferreira Dias
title: Me. Dr.
bio: I am an academic and professional enthusiast in the area of ​​Electrical Engineering - Electronic Systems focused on Artificial Intelligence, I have a Master's and Doctorate from the Federal University of Juiz de Fora and a Bachelor's degree in Mechatronic Engineering from the Federal Institute of Education, Science and Technology of the Southeast of Minas Gerais - Campus Juiz de Fora. Throughout my academic journey, I highlight my participation as a scholarship holder in the Tutorial Education Program, PET-Physics, where I developed comprehensive activities in scientific initiation, teaching, extension, research and innovation. During my undergraduate studies, my focus was on projects involving embedded systems and prototyping, using technologies such as 3D printing and prototyping. My master's research explored the use of Fuzzy Logic for classifying indoor environments using RF signals. Later, I joined the collaboration between ATLAS-Brazil, participating in projects at CERN (European Organization for Nuclear Research) in Geneva. There, I contributed to maintenance and upgrades in FPGA, electronic systems, and conducted research focused on new Artificial Intelligence models. I am currently part of a new working group at ATLAS, where I focus my efforts on research into the application of AI models to improve the performance of electrons and photo triggers in the HLT (High-level Trigger) - the first level of the experiment. Furthermore, I have an incomplete degree in Physics, complementing my multidisciplinary training. I am always looking for challenges that involve innovation and practical application of the knowledge acquired throughout my academic and professional career.

name: Tiago Aparecido Teixeira
title: Me. Dr.
bio: PhD in Electrical Engineering from the Federal University of Juiz de Fora (UFJF) and Master in Computer Science from the Federal University of Viçosa (UFV). He has a specialization in IT Project Management and a degree in Computer Science. He is currently a Systems Analyst at Juiz de Fora City Hall. He has academic and professional experience with an emphasis on Data Science, Artificial Intelligence and Computing Systems. He worked as a researcher in the context of the ATLAS experiment (CERN/LHC), focusing on the development of online filtering systems, energy estimation and signal processing in high-light conditions (High-Luminosity LHC). He has expertise in implementing deconvolution algorithms and Neural Networks on reconfigurable hardware (FPGAs) and coarse-grained architectures. Recently, it expanded its operations to Data Engineering and applied Machine Learning, with complementary training (Bootcamps) and work in the private market (fintechs and international real estate sector), leading predictive model and Generative AI projects.

name: Pedro Henrique Sereno Romeu dos Santos
bio: He has secondary-secondary education at Escola Estadual Professor Sousa Nilo (2019). He has experience in the area of ​​Electrical Engineering, with an emphasis on Electric, Magnetic and Electronic Circuits.

name: Vinicius Pires Diniz de Oliveira
bio: Electrical Engineering student with an emphasis on Telecommunications from the Federal University of Juiz de Fora (UFJF). Since 2023, he has worked as a research fellow associated with CERN's ATLAS experiment, focusing on energy reconstruction, development and validation of detection systems. He worked as a Telecommunications specialist, with experience in VoIP systems, PBX, IPv4/IPv6 networks and large-scale technical support. He worked as an academic monitor at UFJF, helping with teaching support and conducting practical activities. He was Simulations and Research coordinator for the Supernova Rocketry team, leading work on computational modeling, aerodynamic simulation and structural integration. He also worked as a volunteer at the Astronomical Observatory of the UFJF Science Center, assisting in scientific dissemination activities and instrument operation.

name: Fábio Cardani Luna
title: Me.
bio: Electrical engineer, master's degree in Electrical Engineering and currently a PhD candidate, with experience in digital signal processing, digital electronics and FPGA development, with experience applied to high energy physics instrumentation in the ATLAS experiment (CERN). Develops research focused on simulation and processing of signals in real time, with an emphasis on the implementation of digital filters in hardware, including floating point IIR architectures to mitigate quantization errors and improve numerical precision. Has experience in analyzing large-scale experimental data using Python and C++ (ROOT), including .root file processing, histogram generation, statistical studies and performance evaluation of detection systems. Interest in embedded systems, digital signal processing and applications in large scientific experiments.

name: Leandro da Graça Ribeiro
title: Me.
bio: Electrical Engineer with an emphasis on Electronics, currently working at the European Organization for Nuclear Research (CERN). PhD student in Electrical Engineering at the Federal University of Juiz de Fora, focusing on Electronic Systems. He has a technical course in Electronics from Escola Técnica Pandiá Calógeras (2017). He was an intern at the Federal University of Juiz de Fora, where he also completed his undergraduate and master's degrees in Electrical Engineering, with an emphasis on Electronic Systems.

name: Nelson Cevidanes Nascimento de Assis
title: Me.
bio: Master in Telecommunications Engineering from UFF and currently a PhD candidate in Electrical Engineering from UFJF, working at the intersection between programming, electronic engineering and High Energy Physics. My current research covers Instrumentation, Calorimetry, digital signal processing and FPGA implementation. He has experience in developing computational intelligence algorithms, data analysis in Python/R and modeling optical sensor networks. With a consolidated trajectory in the management of technological projects and entrepreneurship in energy systems (Projeta Energias Renováveis), I apply my expertise to develop analytical instrumentation and control electronics solutions aimed at particle accelerators and highly complex environments.
